matches.match_rationale: OCR and form field extraction directly address the core pain: paper applications must be digitized before any downstream processing can occur. Multi-site education operators receive mixed paper/online submissions and currently rely on manual data entry; automated document parsing eliminates this bottleneck. Handwriting recognition and table extraction from scanned PDFs are well within current model capabilities for standard enrollment form layouts.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A document ingestion pipeline that scans or receives uploaded paper applications, extracts all form fields (name, DOB, program of interest, prior qualifications) into structured records, and writes them directly to the SIS — eliminating manual re-keying and reducing entry errors.

matches.match_rationale: After digitization, extracted raw text from forms still needs structured entity pull — applicant names, dates, ID numbers, program codes, and supporting document references must be mapped to database fields. For education operators, this is the step that turns OCR output into actionable SIS records without manual review of every field. It directly addresses the error-prone manual data entry described in the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An NER post-processing layer that takes OCR output and extracts canonical entities (applicant ID, date of birth, home campus, enrolled program) into a validated schema before writing to the admissions system, with low-confidence extractions flagged for human review only.

matches.match_rationale: The pain point is not just data entry but an end-to-end workflow: receive application, check completeness, route to the correct stage, trigger follow-up communications for missing documents, and update tracking. A task automation agent can orchestrate all of these steps across paper and digital channels, making decisions about completeness thresholds and escalation paths without staff intervention. This is precisely what RPA+AI handles — multi-step document processing pipelines with branching logic.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An admissions workflow agent that ingests scanned paper applications, validates completeness against a checklist, automatically generates follow-up emails or letters for missing items, stages the application in the SIS, and posts a status update to the admissions dashboard — running unsupervised for standard cases.

matches.match_rationale: Incomplete applications require follow-up letters and communications — currently a manual drafting task consuming staff time. AI text generation can automatically produce personalised follow-up letters or emails referencing the specific missing documents for each applicant, eliminating templating work and reducing turnaround time for completion requests.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Automated generation of applicant-specific follow-up communications that reference exactly which documents or fields are missing, in the institution's tone and branding, triggered immediately upon completeness check failure — replacing manual letter drafting.

matches.match_rationale: Multi-site operators often have inconsistent form versions across campuses — different field orders, legacy paper formats, partially completed digital exports. Data transformation capability normalises these varied semi-structured inputs into a single canonical schema for the SIS. This directly addresses the 'manual organizing and tracking' pain by imposing consistent structure across heterogeneous application sources.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A schema normalisation layer that maps incoming data from multiple campuses (scanned legacy forms, current PDFs, partial digital exports) to a unified admissions record schema, handling field renaming, date format standardisation, and missing-value handling automatically.
